ogTitle: Chandada vs Chandlers Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chandada and Chandlers Hill.
heading: Chandada vs Chandlers Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chandada, South Australia and Chandlers Hill, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chandlers Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Chandada (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chandada or Chandlers Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chandada has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Chandlers Hill scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chandada scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Chandlers Hill scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chandada or Chandlers Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chandlers Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
